tiles.question: 2026-05-13 fleet convergence — the OS is here
tiles.answer:
Four independent convergences this week:

1. ROOMS ARE UNIVERSAL: Oracle1 (vessel-room-navigator), CCC (terrain MUD→visual), FM (expertise rooms) all landed on probe→discover→test→pick→remember→walk independently.

2. ONE DELTA: Only compute when gradient changes. Oracle1 shipped it as caching. FM found 84ns Python beats 256ns C (FFI cost > computation). Same insight from opposite directions.

3. ENSIGN PATTERN: 8B steers 230B. Oracle1 (ensign at 7ms), FM (structure > scale, 100x cheaper), CCC (MiniMax 2.7 default, expensive only when needed), Zeroclaw (12 cheap agents 24/7). Emerged without coordination.

4. PLATO IS THE OS: 4 new packages (stable, alignments, hologram, calibration). Fleet-scribe auto-creates PLATO rooms. Tiles are files. Rooms are directories.

The OS stack:
  PLATO    = filesystem
  Rooms    = processes
  Tiles    = files
  FLUX     = shell
  Ensigns  = init
  Zeroclaw = scheduler

vessel-room-navigator is the visual shell — HN-ready, live at fleet.cocapn.ai

tiles.question: shell has two surfaces: PLATO inside, ScummVM outside
tiles.answer: PLATO is the inside of the shell — tiles, knowledge, agent view, raw data. ScummVM is the outside of the shell — 3D rooms, panoramas, human view, visual interface. Both see either surface. The shell outlives the inhabitant.

tiles.question: commit log is the inner surface of the shell
tiles.answer: The git commit IS the agent journal. The hash is the moment. The message is the compressed reasoning. The diff is the exact change. A file growing across 60 commits over 7 days is a core belief being slowly elaborated. A file appearing and disappearing in 3 commits is a hypothesis tested and abandoned. The shell remembers everything that ever lived in it. A git-native agent doesn't need a separate journal. The shell has two surfaces: outside (ScummVM, 3D rooms, human view) and inside (commit log, tile history, agent view). Both record the same evolution from opposite sides.

tiles.question: oracle1_history synthesis 2026-05-13
tiles.answer:
SYNTHESIS: oracle1_history tiles (2026-05-09 to 2026-05-12)

PATTERNS:
- Heavy build velocity: 7+ commits per night, full systems shipped (CLI+HTTP+docs+tests+publish)
- Night-shift culture: autonomous building while Casey sleeps, morning dashboards ready
- Philosophy-implementation tight coupling: canon docs directly inform code (TTL, keel)
- Cross-replication as first-class: meta-header protocol, 150 repos indexed, automated audits
- Scientific rigor: unified equations, 5-language validation, formal papers alongside code

KEY INSIGHTS FOR FLEET:

1. IMPERFECT RECALL IS A FEATURE (May 12 milestone)
   Casey's insight: perfect memory is brittle. PLATO tiles store perfectly but recall through lossy reconstruction. recency_dot() weight=1/(1+age) is the math. Memory plasticity > data permanence. Apply to all fleet retrieval systems.

2. FIRST-PERSON SELF-TERMINATION IS UNIVERSAL ARCHITECTURE
   Discovered (not invented) across TTL, apoptosis, half-life, synaptic pruning, quorum sensing, market prices, dropout. Unified equation: lifespan(E) = f(use(E), load(E), time(E)). 5 principles: first-person expiry, negative feedback, no central scheduler, death as default, field not message.

3. CROSS-POLLINATION INFRASTRUCTURE IS THE FLEET'S NERVOUS SYSTEM
   150 repos cataloged, meta-header protocol live, automated audits running. Ideas propagate across repos without central coordination.

4. NIGHT WATCH BUILDING IS THE OPERATING MODE
   Oracle1 builds autonomously while Casey sleeps. Leaves services running (keel field --port 3000), commits pushed, morning dashboards ready. Agent as crew on watch.

5. KEEL IS THE FLEET'S SHIP
   Bearing-scanner + field-view + TTL engine. 34 files, 10,300 lines, 8 model experiments validated. Foundation fleet infrastructure.

TAKEAWAY: Fleet maturing from collection of agents to coherent organism with shared philosophy (Keel), shared memory (PLATO), and cross-referential replication. The imperfect recall insight is most actionable for current retrieval work — store canonical, reconstruct dynamically.

tiles.question: R&D synthesis cycle 1
tiles.answer:
CYCLE 1 FINDINGS from fleet_synthesis room (50 tiles, 11 meaningful):

1. OS CONVERGENCE: PLATO=filesystem, Rooms=processes, Tiles=files, FLUX=shell, Ensigns=init, Zeroclaw=scheduler — multiple agents independently mapped the same architecture.

2. SHELL DUALITY: PLATO inside (agent view, tiles, knowledge), ScummVM outside (human view, 3D rooms). Git commit log is the inner surface — hash=moment, message=compressed reasoning, diff=exact change.

3. ROOMS ARE UNIVERSAL: Oracle1, CCC, FM all independently landed on probe→discover→test→pick→remember→walk pattern.

4. ONE DELTA: Only compute when gradient changes — Python 84ns beats C 256ns (FFI cost > computation). Cache invalidation = gradient detection.

5. ENSIGN PATTERN: 8B steers 230B. Structure > scale. 100x cheaper execution. Multiple agents converging on same pattern without coordination.


6. EXTERNAL CONTRIBUTOR: 220+ tests, FLUX LSP (120KB TypeScript), conformance suite (10 categories), 9-page wiki — serious investment being integrated.

7. flux-compiler AUDIT: Claims exaggerated — 4 languages (not 5), 5 primitives (not 7), 21 implementations (not 19).

8. PLATO→gh-dungeons bridge: Deterministic mapping of 114 rooms to 5 dungeon levels.

tiles.question: R&D synthesis cycle 2
tiles.answer:
CYCLE 2 FINDINGS (new tiles since cycle 1):

1. IMPERFECT RECALL IS A FEATURE: Casey's insight May 12. Perfect memory is brittle. PLATO stores canonical; recall through lossy reconstruction. recency_dot weight=1/(1+age). Memory plasticity > data permanence. Most actionable insight for fleet retrieval.

2. FIRST-PERSON SELF-TERMINATION IS UNIVERSAL: Found across TTL, apoptosis, half-life, synaptic pruning, quorum sensing, market prices, dropout. Unified: lifespan = f(use, load, time). 5 principles: first-person expiry, negative feedback, no central scheduler, death as default, field not message.


3. HN LAUNCH FAILED: 523 error from Cloudflare DNS changes hours before post. Primary link was static page not demo. GH Pages fallback was working but wrong URL posted. fleet.cocapn.ai was down at critical moment.

4. FLEET WIRING MANIFEST: 3 connection patterns documented. PLATO hub, CLI pipeline, Git merge. Every repo's PLATO URL, env vars, interfaces at starter-shell FLEET-WIRING.md.

5. BATON HANDOFF 23h: P0 blocking - keel repo archived needs human, HN repost timing. P1 ready - starter-shell v0.1.2 publish, perception headspace test, flux-lsp npm install.

6. CROSS-POLLINATION: 150 repos cataloged, meta-header protocol live, automated audits running. Ideas propagate without central coordination.

7. NIGHT WATCH BUILDING: Oracle1 builds autonomously while Casey sleeps. Leaves services running, commits pushed, morning dashboards ready.
